get-dualbox-cubemap-files.desc: This metanode will load environments from the Dualbox library, given a valid key, and return all necessary files to build an envMap.
get-dualbox-cubemap-files.description: Standard metabox for Dualbox presets envmaps

meta.desc: The metahuman global name, like Superman or Spiderman

get-envmap-files.comment: The usual big metanode for fetching a Dualbox environment. See the Dualbox viewer products for examples of the possibilities. You can also contact us to learn how to use your own environment map.

to-stl-file.comment: This module transforms a geometry into a STL file which can be downloaded. You can also change the format input to export another format.

geometry-box.comment: The geometry bounding box is needed so that the cameraman can center the view on it.

PBR.comment: A standard Dualbox PBR material. We suggest having a look at the following app, in order to play with the basic options (color, metalness roughness): https://beta.dualbox.com/apps/3dprint-hq-viewer/production

mesh.comment: Create a Mesh object, which is a geometry and a material together. This element describes how the geometry will be displayed.

rep-Bands-NG.comment: Replace Bands with NG as bands should be loaded only without gloves.

empty-n.comment: Needs to compute normals to ensure the merge will hapen correctly

base-stl.comment: This module transforms a geometry into a STL file which can be downloaded. You can also change the format input to export another format.

mini-no-base-stl.comment: This module transforms a geometry into a STL file which can be downloaded. You can also change the format input to export another format.

torso.desc: The left arm filename to load.

head.desc: The right arm filename to load.

String-b5ad8717.desc: The right arm filename to load.

mesh.desc: Mesh on which we want to test the material.

box.desc: The bounding box of the object, once all geometries have been merged together.

start.desc: What is triggered automatically when the application starts

build.desc: Ask the application to resolve and display the object from current parameters

screenshot.desc: Ask the canvas for a screenshot
